page: Microsoft Azure E32-8s_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ESv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The configuration includes 8 dedicated vCPUs derived from 4 physical cores with hyper-threading. It is equipped with 48 MB of L3 cache and a substantial memory capacity of 256.0 GB, yielding 32.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable for remote disk attachments, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks place the E32-8s_v5 consistently in the average range for most operations. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance is recorded at 1899.28, while multi-core performance reaches 7763.24. Memory bandwidth tests show 417.14 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 60.25 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads, alongside a write speed of 43.28 GB/sec. In database and web serving tests, the instance achieves 8.06 million operations per second on Redis SET commands and a static web serving throughput of 12.14 GB/sec. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and larger LLM inference tasks, such as text generation on a 7B model, fall into the lower performance tier.

The primary tradeoff of the E32-8s_v5 is its highly skewed resource allocation, prioritizing memory capacity over raw compute power. With a 32:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio, it offers high resource density for memory-bound applications but may be cost-inefficient for compute-heavy workloads that do not utilize the massive memory footprint. The absence of local storage requires reliance on external premium storage options. This profile is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads such as relational databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, data buffering, and enterprise applications requiring large memory spaces with moderate CPU requirements.
description: The Microsoft Azure E32-8s_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the ESv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is configured with an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, offering 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, and a massive 256.0 GB of RAM. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmark data indicates average performance across standard compute and memory tasks, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 7763.24 and a Redis SET throughput of 8.06 million operations per second. However, multi-threaded decompression and larger LLM inference tasks show weaker relative performance. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32 GB per core, this server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound applications. It is best suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise workloads requiring substantial memory capacity without intensive parallel compute needs.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32-8s_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and Premium Storage capability, designed for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32-8s_v5 is an Intel-based, memory-optimized VM with 8 vCPUs and 256 GB RAM, designed for database and caching workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual machine featuring an Intel Xeon processor and 256 GB of RAM for memory-bound enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 48 MB L3 cache
Massive 256.0 GB RAM configuration providing 32.0 GB per core
Premium Storage capable with no local storage or bundled GPUs
Delivers 8.06 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching, and memory-bound enterprise applications
